📊 Anthropic Economic Index — What The Data Shows

Anthropic's research analyzed millions of real Claude conversations mapped to 20,000+ work tasks. The findings provide the clearest empirical picture of AI's actual impact on jobs — not projections, but observed behavior.

49%
of jobs in sample now use AI for at least 25% of their tasks (up from 36% in Jan 2025)
37.2%
of all Claude queries are computer & mathematical tasks — coding dominates AI usage
52%
of AI conversations are augmentation (human learns/iterates) vs 45% automation
4%
of occupations use AI across 75%+ of their tasks — narrow but deep concentration

Key finding: Workers in highest-risk professions tend to be older, more educated, better-paid. Hiring of younger workers has slowed in exposed fields. Augmentation still dominates, but automation share is gradually growing.

🟢 India's AI Job Opportunity Surge

12 DATA POINTS

Two Sides of the Same Coin

Globally, AI is eliminating roles that haven't adapted. In India specifically, AI is creating more jobs than it's destroying — but only for engineers who upskill. The divide isn't geography. It's capability.

✅ INDIA'S ADVANTAGE
3.8L AI jobs in 2026 · 1.25L new tech roles · Hiring intent +11% · GCC expansion 500K+ positions · Tier-2 cities growing at 25% · 45% demand jump for ML/AI engineers
❌ THE GLOBAL WARNING
91,739 laid off in Q1 2026 · 48% due to AI · 25% of India's entry-level tech jobs at risk (EY) · Skills gap widened from 18% to 25% · 40% of tech skills becoming obsolete
LIMITED SEATS · LAUNCHING SOON
